title: Cosmos DB Persistence Adapter
goal: Create src/persistence.py with a Cosmos DB adapter that initializes the CosmosClient, creates database/container if needed, writes flat arena result records, and provides query functions for leaderboard and history retrieval.

in_scope: Initialize azure.cosmos.CosmosClient using endpoint and credential
Create or get database and container with appropriate partition key
Implement write_arena_result(record) that stores a flat-schema arena result document

Define record schema: arena_id, timestamp, prompt_hash, prompt_text (optional), models_compared, winner, elimination_reasons, inspector_flags_used, model_version, deployment_name, latency, token_usage
Implement query_results(filters) for retrieving historical records
Implement query_leaderboard_data() for aggregation-ready record retrieval
Implement query_prompt_history(limit) for prompt memory retrieval
Generate arena_id using UUID and timestamp using UTC ISO 8601
Generate prompt_hash using hashlib (SHA-256 of prompt text)

inputs: Arena result data from inference and inspector modules
PRD Week 3 section 4 — Cosmos DB persistence
PRD section 8.2 — Data storage and privacy
outputs: src/persistence.py — new module with CosmosClient initialization and CRUD operations
dependencies: 3_persistence_and_memory/Part 01 — Cosmos config keys in config dict
checklist: Implement init_cosmos_client(config) that creates CosmosClient from config endpoint and credential
Implement get_or_create_database(client, database_name) that returns database proxy
Implement get_or_create_container(database, container_name) with partition key on '/deployment_name'
Define ARENA_RESULT_SCHEMA constant documenting all required and optional fields
Implement build_arena_record(arena_data, config) that constructs flat-schema document with all required fields
Set arena_id using uuid.uuid4(), timestamp using datetime.utcnow().isoformat()
If config['persist_prompt_text'] is false, set prompt_text to None in the record
Implement write_arena_result(container, record) that calls container.upsert_item(record)
Implement query_results(container, filters, limit) that queries with optional filters
Implement query_leaderboard_data(container) that retrieves deployment_name, winner, latency fields
Implement query_prompt_history(container, limit) that retrieves distinct prompts ordered by timestamp desc
acceptance_criteria: init_cosmos_client() returns a functional CosmosClient when endpoint and credential are valid
write_arena_result() successfully writes a document with all required schema fields

Persisted schema includes: arena_id, timestamp, prompt_hash, prompt_text (optional), models_compared, winner, elimination_reasons, inspector_flags_used, model_version, deployment_name, latency, token_usage
prompt_hash is a deterministic SHA-256 of the prompt text
prompt_text is omitted (None) when persist_prompt_text is false
query_results() returns filtered records from Cosmos
query_leaderboard_data() returns records with fields needed for leaderboard aggregation
query_prompt_history() returns recent prompts ordered by timestamp descending
No API keys or secret values are written to any Cosmos document
review_focus: Partition key choice ('/deployment_name') supports efficient winner and deployment queries
Record schema is flat and query-friendly as specified in PRD
No secrets or credentials appear in stored documents
CosmosClient initialization uses the correct authentication pattern (key or DefaultAzureCredential)
Query functions use parameterized queries to prevent injection
risks_or_notes: Cosmos SDK may require specific throughput settings — use autoscale or manual 400 RU/s for MVP
Schema drift may occur during iterative development — document version in records if needed later
Partition key design impacts cross-partition query performance — '/deployment_name' is a trade-off
Consider database/container creation permission requirements for the configured credential
Large prompt_text fields may increase document size and cost — optional storage is intentional
definition_of_done: src/persistence.py exists with all listed functions
CRUD operations work against a Cosmos DB instance
No secrets are persisted in any document
Functions are testable with mocked CosmosClient
